title: daucus gingidium subsp. gummifer
intro: Daucus gingidium subsp. gummifer is a sub-species of the Daucus carota plant, commonly known as wild carrot, and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Daucus gingidium subsp. gummifer include relief from menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate skin conditions. Daucus gingidium subsp. gummifer can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
